subject: Re: warp/M in a virtual instance
body:
Hi Pranav,

Here’s our setup:

https://www.embl.de/services/itservices/empowering-science/3D-Cloud/

One basically rents VM’s from IT, these VM’s can always be adapted resource-wise (CPU’s, memory, GPU’s etc.).

Best,

Wim

> On 9. Oct 2020, at 14:50, Pranav Shah <p.shah.lab@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> ﻿
> Hi Dimitry, 
> 
> We are exploring the possibility of running Warp/M in a virtual instance of Windows. Do you think that it is possible?
> 
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
> 
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
